relatedness_reasoning: Inbound file provides eligible customer list with product and profile details; outbound file contains marketing offer scripts targeting those customers. Marketing department receives eligible customers and creates offer scripts for Customer Services delivery—a plausible pipeline step. However, outbound schema focuses on script content and delivery mechanics rather than customer data, limiting direct field correspondence.

mappings.reasoning: No equivalent identifier in inbound; likely system-generated by Marketing for script tracking.
Offer script name likely derived from or references the product name from eligible customer eligibility message.
Inbound target_department is Marketing, which matches the originating department for outbound offer scripts.
Customer Services is the receiving department; not present in inbound schema.
Offer scripts created after eligible customer list is generated; generated_date provides temporal context but is not exact source.
Activation date for offer scripts; not derivable from inbound eligibility message.
Script lifecycle status (active/pending) is not present in inbound schema.
Target segment combines product name and customer allocation strategy to define the cohort for which offer is intended.
Delivery channel (inbound_call, outbound_call, email_template) is not specified in inbound schema.
Verbal delivery duration is not present in inbound customer or product data.
Full script text is generated by Marketing; not present in inbound eligibility data.
Specific call-to-action prompt is created by Marketing; not derivable from inbound schema.
Success KPI (application_completion, appointment_booked, etc.) is not defined in inbound schema.
Number of scripts may correlate with eligible customer count, but is not a direct derivation.
Last updated timestamp relates to when eligible customer list was generated, but represents different temporal concept.
Schema version identifier is not present in inbound data.
